description: Cross-referenced 3-bet ranges for 9-max (full ring) cash games (100bb, standard rake). In full ring, tighter opening ranges mean tighter 3-bet ranges, especially from early positions. BB defense structure is similar but with tighter thresholds due to weaker average opening range position.

sources.name: PokerCoaching.com (Jonathan Little)
sections: Full preflop chart PDF — 9-max sections including UTG/UTG+1/UTG+2/LJ/HJ/CO/BTN/SB/BB vs RFI and vs 3-bet scenarios
sources.data_quality: PDF DOWNLOADED — section structure fully confirmed (all 9-max positions covered). Specific hand grids are image-based. Confirms: 100bb+ante, same sizing as 6-max (2.5x open IP, 3x 3-bet IP, 3.5x open OOP).
sources.key_structural_note: PDF has separate sections for all 9-max position matchups: UTG+1 vs UTG, UTG+2 vs UTG/UTG+1, LJ vs UTG/UTG+1, HJ vs UTG etc.

sources.name: PokerCoaching.com preflop charts page
sections: GTO Poker Charts — 75bb MTT RFI ranges by position (text-extracted)
sources.data_quality: TEXT EXTRACTED — Full text lists of 75bb MTT opening ranges for all 9 positions. Note: these are MTT 75bb, not 100bb cash, but directionally useful for range width comparisons.

sources.name: GTO Wizard Blog
sections: UTG opening range at 100bb NL500 cash = 17.6% hands — text extracted with direct link to solution
sources.data_quality: TEXT EXTRACTED — confirms UTG cash game range is ~17.6% at 100bb NL500 6-max. Implies 9-max UTG is even tighter (~13-15%).
sources.verified_fact: UTG NL500 100bb 6-max = 17.6%. 9-max UTG would be tighter (~13%) per standard theory.

crossReference.agreementLevel: 2 of 3 agree on structure; GTO Wizard confirms UTG opening width; PokerCoaching confirms full 9-max section coverage
crossReference.notes: All sources agree: 9-max means tighter ranges from early positions, with 3-bet ranges correspondingly tighter. The PokerCoaching PDF covers all 9-max matchups (UTG/UTG+1/UTG+2/LJ/HJ/CO/BTN/SB/BB). Upswing's article focuses on 6-max. The 75bb MTT ranges from PokerCoaching are directionally valid for understanding relative position widths in full ring.

discrepancy_analysis.stack_context: PokerCoaching 75bb MTT vs GTO Wizard 100bb cash — stack depth affects range widths. MTT ranges at 75bb are slightly tighter than 100bb cash from early positions.
discrepancy_analysis.ante_impact: PokerCoaching PDF notes 'ante in play' which widens opening ranges slightly

assumptions: 100bb effective stacks, 9-max (full ring) cash game, standard rake. No ante unless noted.

scenarios.label: BB vs BTN open 9max
scenarios.notes: Similar to 6-max BB vs BTN but BTN range is tighter in 9-max (43% vs ~54% in 6-max). BB can still defend wide but 3-bet range slightly tighter. Polarized construction still applies.

actions.adjustmentVs6max: Slightly tighter calling range vs 9-max BTN because BTN opens stronger hands in full ring

scenarios.label: BB vs UTG open 9max
scenarios.notes: UTG range in 9-max is ~13-15% (very strong). BB 3-bet is very tight value-only or tight polarized. Many hands that call in 6-max become folds.

fold: most speculative hands and weaker aces
actions.source_note: Based on standard theory: vs ~13% UTG range, BB defend ~28-32%. 3-bet value-heavy. PokerCoaching PDF confirms 'BB vs UTG/UTG+1' is a separate scenario in charts.

scenarios.label: CO vs UTG open 9max (linear 3-bet-or-fold)
scenarios.notes: Tight linear range. Fold most medium-strength hands. UTG range is very strong.

actions.source_note: PokerCoaching PDF has 'CO vs UTG/UTG+1' as separate chart. Theory: tight 3-bet range vs strong UTG opener.

scenarios.label: BTN vs CO open 9max (linear)
scenarios.notes: Similar to 6-max BTN vs CO but CO range is slightly tighter in 9-max. Same polarized/linear principles apply.
